Title: Interaction with the map
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app
2. Navigate the map to find the data of interest
3. (Optional) Perform a text search to find a specific location quickly
4. (Optional) Select filters to get the type of data desired
Expected Result: The user can view the data of interest on the map

Title: Receiving notifications
Preconditions: The app is installed and running in the background
Test Steps:
1. The app is running in the background
2. A notification is received
Expected Result: The user is notified about the received data

Title: Suggestions
Preconditions: The app is installed and running
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app
2. The app suggests data based on the user's location
Expected Result: The user can view the suggested data

Title: Adding widget
Preconditions: The app is installed and running
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app
2. Add a new widget
3. Configure the widget with the desired data
Expected Result: The widget is added to the app and displays the desired data

Title: Visualize AQI in real-time
Test Steps:
1. Open the application
2. Select a location
3. Check the AQI displayed on the screen
Expected Result: AQI is displayed in real-time for the selected location

Title: Receive notifications
Preconditions: User has allowed the application to send notifications
Test Steps:
1. Check if the application is running
2. Wait for a change in air quality that exceeds a certain threshold
3. Check if a notification is received
Expected Result: A notification is received when there is a change in air quality that exceeds a certain threshold

Title: Implement widget feature
Test Steps:
1. Open the application
2. Navigate to the home screen
3. Select a widget from the application to add to the home screen
4. Select the area and data of interest
5. Check if the selected data is displayed
Expected Result: The selected data is displayed on the home screen

Title: Visualize and update data automatically
Test Steps:
1. Open the application
2. Check if the data is displayed on the screen
3. Wait for the data to update automatically
Expected Result: The data is updated automatically and displayed on the screen

Title: Data update upon user request
Preconditions: User is logged in and has internet connection
Test Steps:
1. User requests data update
2. Application updates data
Expected Result: Data is updated

Title: Deliverables delivery following deadlines
Test Steps: 1. Check if deliverables are delivered following the deadlines set by the professor
Expected Result: Deliverables are delivered following the deadlines

Title: Data removal upon application uninstall
Test Steps:
1. Application is uninstalled
2. Check if all data related to the application is removed
Expected Result: All data related to the application is removed

Title: Interface compatibility across multiple devices
Test Steps: 1. Test the interface on at least 3 different devices
Expected Result: The interface is navigable without errors on all devices

Title: Handling multiple simultaneous requests
Preconditions: Multiple users are using the application simultaneously
Test Steps: 1. Check if the application handles at least 2 simultaneous requests without blocking or losing data
Expected Result: The application handles at least 2 simultaneous requests without blocking or losing data

Title: Error messages upon use of offline features
Preconditions: User tries to use a feature requiring internet connection without internet
Test Steps:
1. User tries to use a feature requiring internet connection without internet
2. Check if the application sends an error message
Expected Result: The application sends an error message

Title: GPS functionality for location determination
Test Steps: 1. Check if the application uses GPS for location determination
Expected Result: The application uses GPS for location determination

Title: System adaptability across platforms and devices
Test Steps: 1. Check if the system can adapt to different platforms and devices, maintaining the same effectiveness and usability
Expected Result: The system can adapt to different platforms and devices, maintaining the same effectiveness and usability

Title: Java and XML code for the project
Test Steps: 1. Check if the project code is written entirely in Java and XML
Expected Result: The project code is written entirely in Java and XML

Title: Future integration of additional features
Test Steps: 1. Check if the application is designed to integrate additional features in future versions
Expected Result: The application is designed to integrate additional features in future versions

Title: Expansion of monitored parameters
Test Steps: 1. Check if the application can expand the set of monitored parameters, including additional pollutants or environmental conditions
Expected Result: The application can expand the set of monitored parameters, including additional pollutants or environmental conditions

Title: Improved access to data through API integration
Test Steps: 1. Check if the application can integrate with additional APIs or local and international datasets to provide more accurate and diverse data
Expected Result: The application can integrate with additional APIs or local and international datasets to provide more accurate and diverse data

Title: Enhanced user interface
Test Steps: 1. Check if future updates to the user interface can make the user experience more intuitive
Expected Result: Future updates to the user interface can make the user experience more intuitive

Title: User can activate notifications
Test Steps:
1. Open the application
2. Navigate to the settings
3. Find and select the notifications option
4. Enable notifications
Expected Result: The user can receive notifications without opening the application

Title: User can add widgets to the home screen
Preconditions: The user has not added a widget
Test Steps:
1. Open the application
2. Navigate to the home screen
3. Find and select the add widget option
4. Choose a widget to add
Expected Result: The user can add widgets to the home screen without having to open the application
